elements.title: Paul Idlout
elements.contents: ... that as a youth, Paul Idlout (pictured) was relocated to the high Arctic by the Canadian government?

elements.title: a golden branch
elements.contents: ... that a golden branch in Virgil's Aeneid inspired a painting by Turner, poems by Yeats, and an anthropological study of ritual murder?

elements.title: Base Set
elements.contents: ... that a box of apparently unopened Base Set card packs was purchased by Logan Paul for $3.5 million, only for it to turn out to be fake?

elements.title: Thandiwe Abdullah
elements.contents: ... that Thandiwe Abdullah created Black Lives Matter in Schools when she was 15 to help promote racial justice?

elements.title: This Monster Wants to Eat Me
elements.contents: ... that imagery of the ocean in This Monster Wants to Eat Me has been interpreted as a visual metaphor for depression?

elements.title: Edward R. Kone
elements.contents: ... that Edward R. Kone had the ugliest face his father had ever seen?

elements.title: Siege of Naples
elements.contents: ... that during the Siege of Naples, local Jews mounted the strongest resistance against the attacking Byzantines in 536?

elements.title: John Ducey
elements.contents: ... that prior to operating the Edmonton Eskimos, baseball executive John Ducey arranged games while an altar boy?

elements.title: a mysterious ghost girl
elements.contents: ... that, when asked about a mysterious ghost girl in Pokémon, producer Junichi Masuda laughed and said "oOoOoOoO"?
